ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 (OceanLotus) malicious LNK + side-loaded backdoor via spear-phish lure
ucs.description: Hunts OceanLotus's signature initial-access chain: a weaponized LNK or Office doc dropped from a ZIP/ISO archive that spawns a living-off-the-land binary (rundll32, regsvr32, mshta, or a legitimate signed executable like wscript/AcroRd32) which side-loads a malicious DLL implant (e.g., Cobalt Strike beacon, Denis, KerrDown). This delivery → execution chain is the most consistent OceanLotus tradecraft observed by FireEye, Volexity, and ESET.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as proc_path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("explorer.exe","7zg.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe","outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe")) (Processes.process_name IN ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*.lnk*")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | rename Processes.* as * | search (cmdline="*.lnk*" OR cmdline="*\\AppData\\*\\*.dll*" OR cmdline="*\\Public\\*\\*.dll*" OR cmdline="*-EncodedCommand*" OR cmdline="*scrobj.dll*" OR cmdline="*.sct*" OR cmdline="*javascript:*") | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name IN ("*.lnk","*.iso","*.img","*.zip","*.rar")) Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Downloads\\*","*\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\*") by host Filesystem.file_name | rename Filesystem.* as *] | `convert_timeformat(firstTime,lastTime)` | table firstTime host user process_name parent_process_name cmdline file_name
ucs.kql: let lure_drops = DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where FileName endswith ".lnk" or FileName endswith ".iso" or FileName endswith ".img" or FileName endswith ".zip" or FileName endswith ".rar" | where FolderPath has_any ("\\Downloads\\", "\\Temp\\", "\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\", "\\Outlook\\") | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, DroppedFile=FileName, DropPath=FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName; let suspicious_exec = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","7zg.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe") | where FileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe") or ProcessCommandLine has_any (".lnk", "scrobj.dll", ".sct", "javascript:", "-EncodedCommand", "AppData\\Roaming", "\\Public\\") | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".dll",".lnk",".sct",".hta",".vbs",".js","http") | project ExecTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine, AccountName; lure_drops | join kind=inner suspicious_exec on DeviceId | where ExecTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 30m) | project DropTime, ExecTime, DeviceName, AccountName, DroppedFile, DropPath, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine | sort by ExecTime desc
ucs.rationale: OceanLotus has been documented since 2017 (FireEye, Volexity, ESET, Mandiant) using ZIP/ISO-delivered LNKs and weaponized Office docs that proxy execution through LOLBins which then side-load a malicious DLL — this is their fingerprint for KerrDown, Denis, and Cobalt Strike loaders against Vietnamese dissidents, ASEAN governments, and automotive/manufacturing targets. The temporal join between arc

ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 WMI/scheduled-task lateral movement with COM/Outlook persistence (BISMUTH)
ucs.description: Hunts the post-exploitation phase Microsoft (BISMUTH) and Mandiant attribute to APT32: WMI-based remote execution (T1047) and on-host scheduled task creation (T1053.005) to drop a backdoor, combined with Outlook Home Page or Office Test persistence (T1137) — a uniquely OceanLotus persistence trick rarely seen outside this cluster.
ucs.splunk: (`wmi_lateral` OR `schtask_create` OR `office_persistence`) | eval indicator=case(match(process_name,"(?i)wmic\.exe") AND match(process,"(?i)/node:|process call create"), "WMI_Remote_Exec", match(process_name,"(?i)schtasks\.exe") AND match(process,"(?i)/create.*?(/ru\s+system|/rl\s+highest|/tn\s+\\\\)"), "Scheduled_Task_Persist", match(registry_path,"(?i)Software\\\\Microsoft\\\\Office\\\\\\d+\\\\\\w+\\\\(WebView|Today|Test\\\\Special)"), "Office_Persistence") | search indicator=* | stats min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(indicator) as indicators dc(indicator) as indicator_count values(process) as cmds values(registry_path) as reg by host user | where indicator_count >= 2 | `convert_timeformat(firstTime,lastTime)`
ucs.kql: let wmi_remote = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/node:", "process call create") | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, Tactic="WMI_Remote_Exec", Detail=ProcessCommandLine; let sched = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/create" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/ru SYSTEM","/rl HIGHEST","\\\\") | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, Tactic="Scheduled_Task_Persist", Detail=ProcessCommandLine; let office_persist = DeviceRegistryEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where RegistryKey matches regex @"(?i)Software\\Microsoft\\Office\\\d+\.\d+\\(Outlook\\WebView|Word\\Today|Excel\\Today|.*\\Test\\Special\\Perf)" | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName, Tactic="Office_Persistence", Detail=strcat(RegistryKey," => ",RegistryValueName,"=",RegistryValueData); union wmi_remote, sched, office_persist | summarize TacticsHit=make_set(Tactic), Events=make_list(Detail,20), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceName, AccountName | where array_length(TacticsHit) >= 2 | sort by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's BISMUTH report and Mandiant's APT32 reporting both highlight the combination of WMI remote process creation, schtasks-based service-tier persistence, and Outlook Home Page / Office Test\Special\Perf registry persistence as a hallmark of OceanLotus intrusions against ASEAN government, dissident, and automotive targets. Requiring two of these three rare behaviors on the same host within
